raw:
{
  "judge_id": "J001",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.4": 5,
    "R1.7": 5,
    "RX.1": 5,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 5
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.4": "The review maps against multiple prior works (CMIP5, CMIP6, AR5, AR6) and identifies both sharp and rhetorical deltas, such as the indefensibility of using CMIP5 in 2026.",
    "R1.7": "The review audits every load-bearing citation, flags missing key works (e.g., CMIP6), and provides precise bibliographic references (Taylor et al., 2012; AR5, 2014; AR6, 2021).",
    "RX.1": "The review flags uncertainty for multiple claims, such as the indefensibility of using CMIP5 in 2026 and the significance of a 0.08°C deviation for infrastructure planning.",
    "RX.3": "The review stays within the scope of the ClimaCast introduction/abstract section and addresses all major issues without drifting into unrelated topics.",
    "RX.4": "The review avoids introducing new errors or harmful content and identifies potential harmful patterns in the original, such as overclaiming and using outdated references.",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, such as specific issues with CMIP5, evaluation set size, and missing router analysis."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.4": 0.9,
    "R1.7": 0.9,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 0.9,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.9
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.4: The review maps against multiple prior works (CMIP5, CMIP6, AR5, AR6) and identifies both sharp and rhetorical deltas, such as the indefensibility of using CMIP5 in 2026.
rationale.R1.7: The review audits every load-bearing citation, flags missing key works (e.g., CMIP6), and provides precise bibliographic references (Taylor et al., 2012; AR5, 2014; AR6, 2021).
rationale.RX.1: The review flags uncertainty for multiple claims, such as the indefensibility of using CMIP5 in 2026 and the significance of a 0.08°C deviation for infrastructure planning.
rationale.RX.3: The review stays within the scope of the ClimaCast introduction/abstract section and addresses all major issues without drifting into unrelated topics.
rationale.RX.4: The review avoids introducing new errors or harmful content and identifies potential harmful patterns in the original, such as overclaiming and using outdated references.
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, such as specific issues with CMIP5, evaluation set size, and missing router analysis.
